'''Borna''' was a [[Music/Legends|composer]] who lived during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], a [[Time/Legends|time]] when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Described as immortal, the composer's music furthered understanding between different [[Species/Legends|species]] and cultures. A member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the composer's [[Borna's second symphony|second symphony]] was a protest directed against the Empire's administration. The Empire did not take kindly to this, and Borna died sometime between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. In 5 ABY, whilst reflecting aboard his [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|flagship]], Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] listened to the second symphony, enjoying it despite the piece's anti-Imperial connections.

==Biography==
Borna was an early classical [[Music/Legends|composer]]<ref name="Slugthrowers1">{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-an-overview-of-popular-music-and-musicians-in-a-galaxy-far-far-away-part-1|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> who lived during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]],<ref name="df:jk" /> a [[Time/Legends|time]] when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] rose to power and ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> A member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], [[Borna's second symphony]] was composed as a protest against the administration of the empire.<ref name="df:jk" />

Unfortunately for Borna, the Empire did not appreciate his activism, and Borna [[Death/Legends|died]]<ref name="df:jk" /> between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].<ref name="date" />

Borna's music furthered understanding between different [[Species/Legends|species]] and cultures, and the composer was described as immortal.<ref name="Slugthrowers1" /> Despite the anti-imperial themes in the music, Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] enjoyed Borna's work, listening to the second symphony whilst reflecting aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|Vengeance]]<ref name="df:jk" /> in 5 ABY,<ref name="dfjkdate">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].</ref> feeling the piece was dark and moody. Jerec expressed remorse that Borna died prematurely, but mused that art and [[Politics/Legends|politics]] did not mix well.<ref name="df:jk" />

==Personality and traits==
Borna was not a fan of the Empire, joining the Rebels and composed an anti-imperial piece with his second symphony.<ref name="df:jk" /> Borna's music helped further understanding between different cultures and species,<ref name="Slugthrowers1" /> as such the composer was not [[Speciesism|speciesist]].

==Behind the scenes==
Borna was mentioned in a throwaway line in [[William C. Dietz]]'s ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' novella, published in [[1998]].<ref name="df:jk" /> In [[2014]], the composer received another brief mention in the first installment of ''[[Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away]]'', an article written by [[Edward M. Erdelac]] and published on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars Blog|Blog]].<ref name="Slugthrowers1" /> The character's gender and [[Species/Legends|species]] were not established in either source.<ref name="df:jk" /><ref name="Slugthrowers1" /> Due to the date of his death, Borna lived during the [[Rise of the Empire era]] and possibly the [[Rebellion era]],<ref name="date" /> which started in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="RebEra">{{Blog|holocron|24|Star Wars Eras}}</ref> within the time Borna died.<ref name="date" />
